disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden pounding headache with profuse sweating and flushing confined to above the chest, in a person with a high spinal cord injury
trigger_question_examples: Where is the sweating and flushing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: His head is pounding and he's sweating and flushed all over his face and chest, but only above the level of his old injury — below that he's pale.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home blood-pressure reading is very high (around 190-195 systolic) with a slow pulse (around 52)
trigger_question_examples: What does the home blood-pressure machine show?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The machine says 190 over 110, and it climbed to about 195 over 115. His pulse is slow — about 52.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known complete high spinal cord injury (T4) with no sensation or movement below the level
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any long-standing medical conditions?
What level is his spinal injury?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He has a complete spinal cord injury at the T4 level — no feeling or movement below his chest — from years ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The indwelling suprapubic catheter has drained very little urine for the past 4 hours
trigger_question_examples: How much is the catheter draining?
When did the catheter bag last fill?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, the catheter bag has hardly filled at all for about the last 4 hours.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On being asked to trace the catheter tubing, the caregiver finds it is kinked/pinched under the patient's clothing, obstructing outflow
trigger_question_examples: Can you trace the whole length of the catheter tube and check for a kink or something pressing on it?
Is the tubing pinched, twisted, or trapped under his clothing or leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: You're right — when I follow the tube down it's kinked and trapped under his clothing. When I straighten it, urine suddenly rushes out.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I didn't think to check the tube — it looked fine from the top.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient feels a sense of panic / impending dread accompanying the physical symptoms
trigger_question_examples: How is he feeling in himself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He keeps saying he feels a terrible sense of panic, like something awful is about to happen.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: Maybe it's just an anxiety attack?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever; the patient is not hot or shivering
trigger_question_examples: Does he have a fever or feel hot and cold?
Any shivering or feeling unwell with infection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, he doesn't feel hot or shivery — his temperature seems normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The emergency under-the-tongue blood-pressure tablet cannot be found by the caregiver
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any emergency blood-pressure medication at home?
Can you find his under-the-tongue tablet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's supposed to have an under-the-tongue tablet for these emergencies but I can't find it anywhere right now.
